seoTitle: Send a QuickBooks Accountant Copy the Right Way
metaDescription: Learn how to send a QuickBooks accountant copy so your accountant can review and adjust your books remotely without disrupting your daily bookkeeping.
intro: Sharing your books with an accountant for tax prep or review shouldn't mean handing over your entire file or risking data conflicts. This guide explains how to send a QuickBooks accountant copy, letting your accountant make adjustments while you keep working. It's built for small business owners who regularly collaborate with an outside accountant or bookkeeper.

steps.heading: Understand why an accountant copy matters
steps.body: An accountant copy lets your accountant review and adjust the books during a specific period without locking you out of daily transactions.

steps.heading: Create the accountant copy
steps.body: Use QuickBooks Online's built-in option to generate a copy of your books for a defined date range.

steps.heading: Send it to your accountant
steps.body: Share the accountant copy directly through QuickBooks or export it so your accountant can access it remotely.

steps.heading: Continue working while it's reviewed
steps.body: Keep entering day-to-day transactions as normal while your accountant works through the copy in parallel.

steps.heading: Import the accountant's changes
steps.body: Once your accountant sends back their adjustments, import them into your live file to merge the changes accurately.

faq.question: Can I still enter transactions while my accountant reviews the copy?
faq.answer: Yes, that's the main benefit — you keep working in your live file while the accountant reviews a separate copy.

faq.question: Will sending an accountant copy risk corrupting my data?
faq.answer: No, this feature is specifically designed to prevent data conflicts between your ongoing work and the accountant's adjustments.

faq.question: How do I merge my accountant's changes back into my file?
faq.answer: QuickBooks provides an import process for the accountant's changes so they merge cleanly into your live books.

faq.question: Is this feature available to beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, though it helps to follow the steps carefully since importing changes incorrectly can create discrepancies.
